title: Lyria 3 Pro Expands to More Google Products for Extended Music Creation
summary: Google's Lyria 3 Pro AI model is now available in more Google products, enabling professionals to create longer music tracks. This expansion integrates AI music generation into daily workflows.
body:
Google has announced the expansion of Lyria 3 Pro, its advanced AI model for music generation, to more of its products. This update allows professionals to create longer music tracks directly within the tools they use every day. The integration aims to streamline the creative process by making AI-driven music composition more accessible.

The inclusion of Lyria 3 Pro in additional Google products marks a significant step forward in AI-assisted music creation. By embedding the model into widely used platforms, Google is democratizing access to sophisticated music generation tools. This move could revolutionize how professionals in various fields, from content creators to marketers, produce and integrate music into their projects.

Looking ahead, the expansion of Lyria 3 Pro is expected to spur further innovation in AI music generation. As more professionals adopt the tool, feedback and usage data will likely drive improvements and new features. The future could see even more seamless integration with other creative software, making AI an indispensable part of the music creation ecosystem.
twitterThread:
🎵 Google just dropped a big one: Lyria 3 Pro is now in more of its products.

You can now create longer tracks directly in the tools you use every day.

This is a game-changer for creators.

Here's why this matters:

Lyria 3 Pro is now embedded in Google's ecosystem. No more switching between apps to generate music.

Professionals can now compose longer tracks seamlessly.

The wild part?

This isn't just for musicians. Marketers, content creators, and more can now easily integrate AI-generated music into their projects.

Let that sink in.

This changes everything for:

- Content creators who need background music
- Marketers looking for quick jingles
- Musicians experimenting with AI

The future of music creation is here.

Hot take:

Google is making AI music generation so accessible, it might soon be as common as using a stock photo.

The bar for entry just dropped.
